close_reason: Deferred: Cow change to core was overwritten by concurrent agent. Core API still uses &'static str. Backends use BACKEND const which is &'static str.
description:
secretx-core/src/lib.rs:986,1009: Both SecretError::Backend and SecretError::Unavailable carry 'backend: &'static str'. This is fine for built-in backends that hand the literal 'aws-sm', 'env', etc. But any future backend whose name is computed at runtime — e.g. a 'pkcs11:LIBPATH' meta-backend that wraps a dynamic library, or a generic 'http:URL' fetcher — has to Box::leak its name to satisfy &'static, which is a memory leak per error.

Trigger: it's not hypothetical — the existing pkcs11 backend already has a configurable library path that distinguishes one backend instance from another in error reporting; today the static label hides that distinction.

Fix: change to Cow<'static, str> or simply &str + the lifetime parameter you'd need (more invasive). Cow is the conservative choice: callers that pass a string literal get exactly today's behaviour with zero cost; callers that pass an owned String pay one allocation at error-construction time, which is acceptable for an error path.
notes: Cow change was overwritten by concurrent agent editing core. Reverted backend crates to use plain &'static str. Core API change deferred.
title: SecretError::Backend.backend: &'static str rules out runtime-derived backend names
